reps.visible_state: Hero is Big Blind versus Button on an A-c 7-d 2-s dry flop, has a weak hand with no strong draw, and Button bets around half pot.
reps.learner_prompt: You are out of position with a weak hand on a dry ace-high board. What is the best one-focus decision?
reps.correct_feedback: Good fold. Position and board texture make this a poor spot to continue with a weak hand.
reps.incorrect_feedback: Continuing here overvalues a weak hand out of position. Re-check who has the board advantage and whether you have a real draw.
reps.repair_cue: Re-check position, board advantage, and draw quality.

reps.visible_state: Hero is Button versus Big Blind on a J-h 9-h 4-c connected two-tone flop, has an open-ended straight draw, and Big Blind leads around one-quarter pot.
reps.learner_prompt: You have position, a real draw, and face a small bet. What is the best one-focus decision?
reps.correct_feedback: Good continue. The price is small and your draw has a clear improvement path.
reps.incorrect_feedback: Folding gives up too much when the price is small and your draw is real. Re-check the bet size and your improvement cards.

reps.visible_state: Hero is Cutoff versus Big Blind on a Q-s Q-d 6-s paired two-tone board, has only a weak backdoor draw and no pair, and a straightforward Big Blind leads around three-quarters pot.
reps.learner_prompt: You have only a weak backdoor draw and face a large bet. What is the best one-focus decision?
reps.correct_feedback: Good fold. A weak backdoor draw is not enough when the price is large.
reps.incorrect_feedback: This call chases hope, not a real draw. Re-check draw quality before reacting to the board texture.
reps.repair_cue: Re-check whether this is a real draw or only backdoor hope.

reps.visible_state: Hero is Button versus Small Blind on an 8-c 5-d 2-s low dry flop, has two overcards and backdoor potential, and a Small Blind who bluffs too often bets around one-quarter pot.
reps.learner_prompt: Villain bluffs too often, bets small, and you have position. What is the best one-focus decision?
reps.correct_feedback: Good continue. Player tendency and price make this a spot where folding too quickly gives up value.
reps.incorrect_feedback: Folding ignores the opponent's tendency. Re-check whether the small bet comes from a player who bluffs too often.

reps.visible_state: Hero is Hijack versus Big Blind on a K-d T-d 8-c coordinated high-card flop, has ace-high with no strong draw, and a tight Big Blind check-raises after Hero bets small.
reps.learner_prompt: A tight player check-raises you on a connected high-card board. You have ace-high and no strong draw. What is the best one-focus decision?
reps.correct_feedback: Good fold. The player tendency and action both point to strength.
reps.incorrect_feedback: Continuing ignores the strongest visible signal: a tight player check-raising on a connected board.
reps.repair_cue: Re-check whether this action is usually strength.

reps.visible_state: Hero is Button versus Big Blind on a 9-c 6-d 2-s semi-dry middle-card flop, has middle pair, and a Big Blind who calls wide bets around one-quarter pot.
reps.learner_prompt: You have position, a reasonable made hand, and face a small bet from a wide player. What is the best one-focus decision?
reps.correct_feedback: Good continue. You have position, a fair price, and a hand that can still win at showdown.
reps.incorrect_feedback: Folding is too tight here. Re-check your position, the small price, and your showdown value.
reps.repair_cue: Re-check position, price, and showdown value.
